behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 Agentic: correctness depends on real speech-to-text model output (speaker diarization), so a stubbed transcript can hide real mislabeling (LLM-DEP + MOCK-HIDE). REAL-INFRA exists (external API), but the core failure is model output quality, not transport. PASS2 counter-argument (System) rejected because behavior text is about "preserves speaker labels," i.e., model decision quality, not HTTP/subprocess behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run real meeting audio through the real STT API and assert diarized speaker labels in transcript map correctly to known attendees sufficiently for downstream attribution.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 Unit: this behavior is deterministic parsing/transformation of transcript text into schema fields (MOCK-MISS says failure is local logic). No required OS/network/subprocess, and no dependence on model judgment if transcript text is provided as fixture. PASS2 counter-argument (Integration) rejected because behavior text scopes to "Transcript parsing extracts... into the summary input structure," which can be validated at one parser/object boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser fixture transcripts containing explicit assignment phrases and assert extracted `{assignee, task_text}` objects are produced in the summary-input structure, including edge phrasing cases.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 Agentic: this is about summary output completeness/attribution quality from real LLM generation; deterministic mocks would always pass and miss omission failures (LLM-DEP + deterministic-assertion trap). PASS2 counter-argument (Workflow) rejected because behavior text targets one summary output condition, not explicit multi-stage agentic loop correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Use real transcript inputs with known assigned attendees, call the real summarization LLM, and assert each assigned attendee appears with at least one action item in structured output.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 Unit: phrased as "logic routes... into schema," indicating deterministic mapping/routing code after decisions are identified; failure boundary is local transformation. Integration brake: no real OS/network/subprocess, no required model-quality check, no multi-agent chain. PASS2 counter-argument (Agentic) rejected because behavior text does not require testing whether model identifies decisions, only where identified decisions are routed.
behaviors.test_description: Given input objects already marked as decisions, assert routing code places them under `decisions` schema and not narrative fields across representative payload variants.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 Agentic: this checks whether real model output captures and classifies decision content correctly into the right section; stubbed generations hide this failure (LLM-DEP + MOCK-HIDE). PASS2 counter-argument (Integration) rejected because behavior text is outcome-quality oriented ("Key decisions... appear"), not just routing plumbing.
behaviors.test_description: Run real transcripts containing explicit decision statements through real summarization, then verify those decisions are present in `decisions` and absent as narrative-only mentions.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 Workflow: requires multiple sequential agentic steps (per-segment STT/summary + merge), and correctness depends on cross-step handoff and aggregation (MULTI-STEP). Integration/System alone under-test coherence and accumulation across repeated model decisions. PASS2 counter-argument (Agentic) rejected because behavior explicitly requires segmented sequential processing and merged final output, i.e., chained agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real >60-minute recording, execute actual segmentation pipeline end-to-end across all segments, and assert final merged summary preserves segment coverage, consistent attendee/action-item continuity, and a single coherent structured result.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 Workflow: explicit full pipeline with real recording spans multiple stages including AI calls; failure can emerge from inter-stage orchestration plus model-dependent steps. By definition this is multi-step agentic/system execution. PASS2 counter-argument (System) rejected because behavior text is not just infra execution; it includes transcribe and summarize stages whose outputs govern downstream success.
behaviors.test_description: Run a production-like end-to-end job on a real meeting file from upload through delivery, asserting unattended completion, valid structured schema, and successful stage-to-stage state propagation.
